author: omerfirmak
content:
Hey everyone, I have been working on a EIP-7928 impl and have a quick question.

wrt delegations, spec says:

```
EIP-7702 Delegations: The delegation target MUST NOT be included during delegation creation or modification **and MUST only be included once it is actually loaded as an execution target** (e.g., via CALL/CALLCODE/DELEGATECALL/STATICCALL under authority execution).
```

but in `tests/amsterdam/eip7928_block_level_access_lists/test_block_access_lists_opcodes.py::test_bal_call_revert_insufficient_funds[fork_Amsterdam-call_opcode_CALL-blockchain_test-delegated-cold_target-cold_delegation]` a CALL to an account with delegation fails due to the caller not having enough funds for the value transfer. But BAL still includes the authority address, even tho the code at authority was never needed to be read. I'm wondering if this was ever discussed?
